extracted_json.title: Structural Analysis of Industry (Competitive Strategy Analysis)

blocks.text: Structural Analysis of Industry (aka Competitive Strategy Analysis)
Competitive Strategy comes from "The Positioning School" (Porter ≈ 1980)

items.text: Industry: The set of players for some technology or product type, e.g., disk-drive industry, consumer electronics, health-care industry, ....
Players (in the industry): Competitors, new entrants, substitutes, suppliers
Market: Buyers (or customers) of the product/service
Forces: The micro-economic forces that exist among the players.
Company (or Firm or Enterprise): is embedded in the industry of interest

extracted_json.summary_topic_line: Terminology of Porter's structural/competitive strategy analysis of industry
extracted_json.summary_gist: Introduces structural analysis of industry (competitive strategy analysis), attributed to Porter's "Positioning School" (~1980), and defines key terminology: industry, players, market, forces, and company/firm/enterprise.

transcription:
3. Structural Analysis of Industry (aka
      Competitive Strategy Analysis)
            Competitive Strategy comes from
   "The Positioning School" ("Porter ≈ 1980)

      Terminology :

      Industry : The set of players for some
                 technology or product type, e.g.,
                 disk-drive industry, consumer electronics,
                 health-care industry, ....

      Players (in    : Competitors, new entrants,
      the industry)     substitutes, suppliers

      Market : Buyers (or customers) of the
               product/service

      Forces : The micro-economic forces that
               exist among the players.

      Company (or Firm or Enterprise) : is embedded in
                                        the industry of
                                        interest
